domain: Autonomous Grid Control / Smart Grid / AI Control

subspaces.name: Distributed Energy Resource Coordination
subspaces.description: Real-time orchestration of heterogeneous distributed energy resources (solar PV, batteries, EVs, flexible loads) to maintain grid stability and optimize local economics.
seed_papers: IEEE PES Report on DER Integration (2022)
Kroposki et al., 'Grid-Forming Inverters: A Critical Asset for the Power Grid', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2019
subspaces.probe_query: distributed energy resources inverter-based resource control grid-edge coordination virtual power plant DER aggregation flexibility markets

subspaces.name: Grid-Forming Control
subspaces.description: Control paradigm enabling inverter-based resources to autonomously establish voltage and frequency without relying on synchronous machines—foundational for black-start and high-renewables grids.
seed_papers: Ulbig et al., 'Analysis, Modeling, and Validation of Grid-Forming Inverter Dynamics', IEEE Trans. Power Systems, 2021
Baker et al., 'Grid-Forming Inverters: A Critical Asset for the Power Grid', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2019
subspaces.probe_query: grid-forming inverters synchronverter virtual oscillator control droop-free synchronization inertia emulation stable islanding

subspaces.name: Cyber-Physical Security for Grid Control
subspaces.description: Protection of real-time control loops against adversarial manipulation—including false data injection, sensor spoofing, and coordinated attacks targeting AI-driven decision modules.
seed_papers: Liu et al., 'False Data Injection Attacks Against State Estimation in Power Systems', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2011
Sridhar et al., 'Cyber-Physical Systems Security for the Smart Grid', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2012
subspaces.probe_query: false data injection attack secure state estimation resilient control cyber-physical co-simulation attack detection in SCADA/EMS AI robustness for grid control

subspaces.name: Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Grid Optimization
subspaces.description: Application of model-free and model-based reinforcement learning to learn optimal dispatch, voltage regulation, and fault recovery policies under stochastic renewable generation and load dynamics.
seed_papers: Zhang et al., 'Deep Reinforcement Learning for Power System Optimization and Control: A Review', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2023
Meng et al., 'Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Distributed Voltage Control', Nature Energy, 2022
subspaces.probe_query: reinforcement learning power systems online grid optimization RL for AC OPF adaptive control policy multi-agent RL grid sample-efficient RL control

subspaces.name: Digital Twin for Grid Operations
subspaces.description: High-fidelity, synchronized virtual representation of physical grid assets and dynamics, integrating physics-based models, sensor data, and AI for predictive control, scenario testing, and closed-loop automation.
seed_papers: Wang et al., 'Digital Twin in Power Systems: Architecture, Applications, and Challenges', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2022
NISTIR 8376: Digital Twins for Smart Grids, 2022
subspaces.probe_query: digital twin power system real-time grid simulation co-simulation platform model-data fusion twin-enabled control synchrophasor-integrated twin

subspaces.name: Autonomous Microgrid Operation
subspaces.description: Self-sufficient operation of microgrids—including island detection, seamless transition, internal stability maintenance, and peer-to-peer energy trading—without centralized utility commands.
seed_papers: Guerrero et al., 'Hierarchical Control of Droop-Controlled AC and DC Microgrids', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2011
Zhao et al., 'Autonomous Microgrid Control: From Theory to Practice', IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, 2022
subspaces.probe_query: microgrid autonomy islanding detection plug-and-play control peer-to-peer energy trading microgrid stability hierarchical microgrid control

subspaces.name: Physics-Informed Machine Learning for Grid Dynamics
subspaces.description: Integration of first-principles power system models (e.g., swing equations, power flow constraints) into ML architectures to ensure physical consistency, interpretability, and generalization in control and forecasting tasks.
seed_papers: Rahhal et al., 'Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Power System Dynamics', IEEE Trans. Power Systems, 2023
Chen et al., 'Neural Ordinary Differential Equations for Dynamic Power System Modeling', arXiv:2205.04371
subspaces.probe_query: physics-informed neural networks PIML power systems constrained neural ODE structured deep learning embedded differential equations learnable power flow

subspaces.name: Wide-Area Adaptive Protection and Control
subspaces.description: Protection and control schemes leveraging time-synchronized PMU data across transmission corridors to detect faults, assess system stress, and trigger adaptive corrective actions faster than conventional relay logic.
seed_papers: Phadke & Thorp, 'Synchronized Phasor Measurements and Their Applications', IEEE Press, 2008
Jiang et al., 'Wide-Area Adaptive Protection Using Synchrophasor Data', IEEE Trans. Power Delivery, 2019
subspaces.probe_query: wide-area protection PMU-based control adaptive relaying system integrity protection scheme fault location using synchrophasors dynamic stability assessment

subspaces.name: AI-Enabled Distribution System State Estimation
aliases: 人工智能配网状态估计
subspaces.description: High-resolution, real-time estimation of voltages, currents, and topology in unobservable low-voltage distribution networks using sparse sensor data, smart meter analytics, and graph neural networks.
seed_papers: Huang et al., 'Graph Neural Networks for Distribution System State Estimation', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2022
Zhou et al., 'Deep Learning-Based Distribution System State Estimation: A Review', IEEE Access, 2023
subspaces.probe_query: distribution system state estimation GNN for DSE smart meter data fusion topology identification unobservable feeder estimation probabilistic DSE

subspaces.name: Resilient Multi-Timescale Control Architecture
subspaces.description: Integrated control framework spanning sub-second (stability), second-to-minute (dispatch), and minute-to-hour (economic optimization) timescales with cross-layer coordination, failure isolation, and graceful degradation.
seed_papers: Ding et al., 'A Resilient Hierarchical Control Architecture for Future Power Grids', IEEE Trans. Smart Grid, 2021
Chowdhury et al., 'Multi-Timescale Coordinated Control of Renewable-Dominated Power Systems', IEEE Trans. Power Systems, 2023
subspaces.probe_query: multi-timescale control hierarchical grid control resilient control architecture cross-layer coordination failure-aware control adaptive time horizon scheduling

user_interaction.question: Select the subspaces to prioritize before ZhiZhi imports papers. You can also add custom subspaces.

options.label: Distributed Energy Resource Coordination
options.description: Real-time orchestration of heterogeneous distributed energy resources (solar PV, batteries, EVs, flexible loads) to maintain grid stability and optimize local economics.

options.label: Grid-Forming Control
options.description: Control paradigm enabling inverter-based resources to autonomously establish voltage and frequency without relying on synchronous machines—foundational for black-start and high-renewables grids.

options.label: Cyber-Physical Security for Grid Control
options.description: Protection of real-time control loops against adversarial manipulation—including false data injection, sensor spoofing, and coordinated attacks targeting AI-driven decision modules.

options.label: Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Grid Optimization
options.description: Application of model-free and model-based reinforcement learning to learn optimal dispatch, voltage regulation, and fault recovery policies under stochastic renewable generation and load dynamics.

options.label: Digital Twin for Grid Operations
options.description: High-fidelity, synchronized virtual representation of physical grid assets and dynamics, integrating physics-based models, sensor data, and AI for predictive control, scenario testing, and closed-loop automation.

options.label: Autonomous Microgrid Operation
options.description: Self-sufficient operation of microgrids—including island detection, seamless transition, internal stability maintenance, and peer-to-peer energy trading—without centralized utility commands.

options.label: Physics-Informed Machine Learning for Grid Dynamics
options.description: Integration of first-principles power system models (e.g., swing equations, power flow constraints) into ML architectures to ensure physical consistency, interpretability, and generalization in control and forecasting tasks.

options.label: Wide-Area Adaptive Protection and Control
options.description: Protection and control schemes leveraging time-synchronized PMU data across transmission corridors to detect faults, assess system stress, and trigger adaptive corrective actions faster than conventional relay logic.

options.label: AI-Enabled Distribution System State Estimation
options.description: High-resolution, real-time estimation of voltages, currents, and topology in unobservable low-voltage distribution networks using sparse sensor data, smart meter analytics, and graph neural networks.

options.label: Resilient Multi-Timescale Control Architecture
options.description: Integrated control framework spanning sub-second (stability), second-to-minute (dispatch), and minute-to-hour (economic optimization) timescales with cross-layer coordination, failure isolation, and graceful degradation.

custom_subspace_input.placeholder: e.g. Demand Response; EV Charging Coordination; Building Energy Management
custom_subspace_input.instructions: If your target subfield is not listed, provide one subspace per line or semicolon-separated. These will be converted into custom retrieval branches.

user_interaction.continue_with: Pass subspace_map_id plus selected_subfields, or pass option labels as focus_branches to run_zhizhi_literature_analysis.
